selected_important_turn.text: Well I will definitely need 2 packages of food and 2 of firewood for survival.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first firm, specific claim by YOU that directly competes with THEM's earlier demand for food and firewood. It anchors the speaker's entitlement and shifts the interaction toward a zero-sum frame, shaping subsequent concessions.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It's rigid and framed as a survival minimum rather than an opening for tradeoffs. That rigidity reduces integrative possibilities and misses the chance to reciprocate or probe for mutually beneficial exchanges after THEM's justificatory turn.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the anchor and convert the demand into an invitational proposal or conditional request (e.g., state the need but immediately suggest tradeable concessions or ask what they'd accept in return), or ask clarifying questions that seek their priorities to open integrative options.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by opening space for tradeoffs rather than hard-anchoring)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: improve (by signaling willingness to negotiate specifics rather than claiming an absolute minimum)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (reduces friction and creates clearer pathways to compromise)

value2reason.Low: Water is essential to survival. It is necessary to drink, cook and bathe in.
value2reason.Medium: Firewood could get wet and we would need more to stay warm at night and on cold days. It is also important for cooking.
value2reason.High: We will be hungry and food is essential for survival.

value2reason.Low: While we can make fire with what we have, additional wood would be good in case what we have gets wet
value2reason.Medium: While we do have some water, the weather is looking to be especially hot and we will probably need more as we are looking to do a lot of hiking and kayaking.
value2reason.High: I have two teenage boys who require a lot of food, especially when expending so much energy with all the activities of camping.

dialogue.text: Hi.  I am looking at possibly taking 3 of the food packages, 2 of the water packages and one of the firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Hi. Why do you need so many food packages?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Well, I have two teenage boys who will be with me.  In normal circumstances they go through a lot of food.  While camping, hiking and kayaking I know we will need plenty to keep their energy level high to do everything.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Well I will definitely need 2 packages of food and 2 of firewood for survival.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 food packages to ensure we have enough for the trip. What would you be willing to trade for them? Maybe firewood or water?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m willing to give you 1 food package if you provide 2 water packages. That way, I can secure more food for my boys, and you get extra water for the hot weather.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm open to trading 1 food package for 2 water packages, but I'd also like to ensure we both have enough firewood. What's your take on splitting the firewood 2-1 in your favor?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I’m okay with the food and water trade, but I’d prefer 2 firewood packages for you and 1 for me. It’s better to have more firewood in case it gets wet, and I can manage with less food if needed.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m happy with the food and water trade, but let’s confirm the firewood split. If you take 2 packages, I’ll take 1. That way, we both have enough for the trip, and we’re good to go.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed! I’ll take 1 food, 2 water, and 1 firewood. You get 2 food, 1 water, and 2 firewood. Safe and sound for the trip!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
